---
title: "gpd_pvalue"
generated: true
description: "Tail-accelerated exceedance p-value via a generalised Pareto fit."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.randomise`](../nitrix-stats-inference-randomise.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gpd_pvalue -->
```python
gpd_pvalue(stat: Float[Array, '...'], null_dist: Float[Array, 'n'], *, n_exceedances: int = 250) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gpd_pvalue -->
Tail-accelerated exceedance p-value via a generalised Pareto fit.

The permutation FWE p-value of an observed statistic $T$ is the
survival fraction $P(\max_{\mathrm{null}} \geq T)$.  Estimated
empirically it is discrete and floored at $1 / n$ -- so resolving a
small p-value needs a large $n$.  Following Winkler et al. (2016;
see the module references), the upper tail of `null_dist` above a
threshold $u$ (the $(k+1)$-th largest of $n$, with
$k =$ `n_exceedances`) is modelled by a **generalised Pareto
distribution** fitted by the method of moments, giving a smooth p-value
that can fall below $1 / n$ at a fraction of the permutations:

.. list-table:
   :widths: auto

   * - $P(T) = (k / n)\, S_{\mathrm{GPD}}(T - u)$
     - $T > u$ (tail, GPD survival)
   * - $P(T) = \#\{\mathrm{null} \geq T\} / n$
     - $T \leq u$ (body, empirical)

The GPD shape $\xi$ and scale $\sigma$ are recovered from the
exceedance mean and variance,
$\xi = (1 - \mathrm{mean}^2 / \mathrm{var}) / 2$ and
$\sigma = \mathrm{mean}\,(1 - \xi)$.  The routine is pure and
differentiable, and requires no dense factorisation library.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gpd_pvalue -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `stat` | `Float` | `...` | — | The observed statistic(s) whose FWE p-value(s) are wanted. A scalar or an array of arbitrary shape (e.g. an enhanced FWE statistic map). |
| `null_dist` | `Float` | `n` | — | The `(n,)` null distribution of the spatial-maximum statistic (one value per permutation). |
| `n_exceedances` | `int` | — | `250` | The number of upper-tail exceedances $k$ used to fit the GPD (default `250`). Clamped to at most `n - 1`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. The FWE p-value(s), the same shape as `stat`: the GPD-based tail estimate where `stat` exceeds the threshold $u$, and the empirical fraction otherwise.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gpd_pvalue -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
